mnemonics.title: INSTRUCTION CYCLE STEPS
mnemonics.content: F DES (FETCH-DECODE-EXECUTE-STORE) - THE PROCESSOR LEARNS TO EXECUTE PROGRAMS DAILY
CAUSE (CONTROL UNIT, ALU, REGISTERS, EXECUTION) - A CPU REALLY SIMPLE
mnemonics.title: CPU COMPONENTS
MEMORY HIERARCHY
mnemonics.content: RACY (REGISTERS, CACHE, MAIN MEMORY, SECONDARY STORAGE) - RAPID ACCESS YIELDS SPEED

mnemonics.title: SYSTEM BUS TYPES
mnemonics.content: ADC (ADDRESS BUS, DATA BUS, CONTROL BUS) - ALWAYS DATA CONTROLS MOVEMENT

acronyms.usage: Performs arithmetic and logical operations in the CPU
Holds address of next instruction to be executed
Holds memory address for read/write operations
Holds data being transferred to/from memory
Generates control signals to coordinate processor operation
Allows devices to transfer data without CPU intervention
CPU architecture with simple, few instructions
CPU architecture with complex, many instructions
Communication between CPU and peripheral devices

visualTips.visualization: DRAW A CIRCLE WITH FOUR QUADRANTS LABELED FETCH, DECODE, EXECUTE, STORE. ARROW FROM EACH PHASE TO THE NEXT TO SHOW THE CYCLIC NATURE.
DRAW AN INVERTED PYRAMID: SMALL TOP (REGISTERS), MEDIUM SECTION (CACHE), LARGE SECTION (RAM), WIDE BOTTOM (SECONDARY). LABEL EACH LEVEL WITH ACCESS TIME INCREASING DOWNWARD.
DRAW A BOX WITH ALU AT CENTER, CONTROL UNIT ON TOP, REGISTERS ON ONE SIDE, BUSES CONNECTING THEM. SHOW DATA FLOW WITH ARROWS.
DRAW TWO CIRCLES: INNER CIRCLE = KERNEL MODE (FULL ACCESS), OUTER RING = USER MODE (RESTRICTED). SHOW TRANSITION ARROWS WHEN SYSTEM CALLS OCCUR.

analogies.analogy: THE CPU IS LIKE THE BRAIN OF A COMPUTER - IT RECEIVES INSTRUCTIONS, PROCESSES THEM, AND CONTROLS ALL OTHER COMPONENTS.
CACHE IS LIKE A DESK DRAWER NEAR YOUR WORK AREA - SMALL BUT FAST ACCESS TO FREQUENTLY USED ITEMS, WHILE MAIN MEMORY IS LIKE A FILING CABINETER AWAY FROM THE DESK.
THE SYSTEM BUS IS LIKE A CITY'S ROAD NETWORK - ADDRESS BUS IS LIKE STREET NAMES, DATA BUS IS LIKE VEHICLES CARRYING PASSENGERS, CONTROL BUS IS LIKE TRAFFIC LIGHTS AND SIGNS.
AN INTERRUPT IS LIKE A PHONE RINGING - IT PAUSES YOUR CURRENT ACTIVITY (WHETHER YOU'RE POLLING OR NOT) TO ANSWER THE CALL, THEN YOU RETURN TO WHAT YOU WERE DOING.
DMA IS LIKE A PERSONAL ASSISTANT WHO CAN DELIVER DOCUMENTS DIRECTLY TO COLLEAGUES WITHOUT CONSTANTLY ASKNG YOU TO SUPERVISE - THEY WORK INDEPENDENTLY WHILE YOU FOCUS ON OTHER TASKS.
KERNEL MODE IS LIKE A MANAGER'S OFFICE WITH KEYS TO ALL ROOMS, WHILE USER MODE IS LIKE A STANDARD EMPLOYEE WITH ACCESS TO ONLY THEIR OWN DESK AND COMMON AREAS.
